ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.
M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Did you feel that even if you might have moments of doubt, confusion, or mistrust, that overall, therapy is worthwhile?
Notes: 1 = Never/Not at all; 2 = A little bit; 3 = Somewhat; 4 = Moderately; 5 = Quite a bit; 6 = Quite a lot; 7 = Very much so

ElementDescription: When important things came to mind, how often did you find yourself keeping them to yourself rather than sharing them with your therapist?
Notes: 1 = Never/Not at all; 2 = A little bit; 3 = Somewhat; 4 = Moderately; 5 = Quite a bit; 6 = Quite a lot; 7 = Very much so

ElementDescription: Did you feel accepted and respected by your therapist for who you are?
Notes: 1 = Never/Not at all; 2 = A little bit; 3 = Somewhat; 4 = Moderately; 5 = Quite a bit; 6 = Quite a lot; 7 = Very much so

ElementDescription: How much did you hold back your feelings during this session?
Notes: 1 = Never/Not at all; 2 = A little bit; 3 = Somewhat; 4 = Moderately; 5 = Quite a bit; 6 = Quite a lot; 7 = Very much so

ElementDescription: Did you feel that you were working together with your therapist; that the two of you were joined in a struggle to overcome your problem?
Notes: 1 = Never/Not at all; 2 = A little bit; 3 = Somewhat; 4 = Moderately; 5 = Quite a bit; 6 = Quite a lot; 7 = Very much so

ElementDescription: During this session, how dedicated was your therapist to helping you overcome your difficulties?
Notes: 1 = Never/Not at all; 2 = A little bit; 3 = Somewhat; 4 = Moderately; 5 = Quite a bit; 6 = Quite a lot; 7 = Very much so

ElementDescription: How much did you resent the time, cost, or other demand of your therapy?
Notes: 1 = Never/Not at all; 2 = A little bit; 3 = Somewhat; 4 = Moderately; 5 = Quite a bit; 6 = Quite a lot; 7 = Very much so

ElementDescription: Did you feel that your therapist understood what you hoped to get out of this session?
Notes: 1 = Never/Not at all; 2 = A little bit; 3 = Somewhat; 4 = Moderately; 5 = Quite a bit; 6 = Quite a lot; 7 = Very much so

ElementDescription: How much did you find yourself thinking that therapy was not the best way to get help with your problems?
Notes: 1 = Never/Not at all; 2 = A little bit; 3 = Somewhat; 4 = Moderately; 5 = Quite a bit; 6 = Quite a lot; 7 = Very much so

ElementDescription: Did the treatment you received in this session match with your ideas about what helps people in therapy?
Notes: 1 = Never/Not at all; 2 = A little bit; 3 = Somewhat; 4 = Moderately; 5 = Quite a bit; 6 = Quite a lot; 7 = Very much so

ElementDescription: Did you have the impression that you were unable to deepen your understanding of what it is bothering you?
Notes: 1 = Never/Not at all; 2 = A little bit; 3 = Somewhat; 4 = Moderately; 5 = Quite a bit; 6 = Quite a lot; 7 = Very much so

ElementDescription: How much did your therapist help you gain a deeper understanding of your problems?
Notes: 1 = Never/Not at all; 2 = A little bit; 3 = Somewhat; 4 = Moderately; 5 = Quite a bit; 6 = Quite a lot; 7 = Very much so
